title: spergula segetalis
intro: Spergula segetalis, also known as Corn Spurrey or Small Spurrey, is a small annual herb belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various digestive issues such as diarrhea and dysentery. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in reducing swelling and preventing infections, thereby providing health benefits like soothing digestive issues and promoting wound healing. Spergula segetalis can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and it's often combined with other herbs in traditional herbal medicine to enhance its effects.
